texts.full_text: : First Punic War : article by Polybius of Megalopolis Polybius: the First Punic War According to the Greek historian Polybius of Megalopolis (c.200-c.118), the First Punic War (264-241) between Carthage and Rome was "the longest and most severely contested war in history". And indeed, it lasted almost a quarter of a century and probably, a million people lost their lives. In the end, Rome had conquered the island of Sicily, and had become a Mediterranean superpower. Polybius' World History was translated by W.R. Paton. Mamertine coin, showing the war god and a bull (©!!) Like the four craft breweries that call the valley home, Haughey aims to offer something for everyone: pilsners, IPAs, sours. This is not Denver, where you need to build a niche to stand out in a crowded market. She only buys one keg of everything, so taps constantly rotate. A few things stood out about the festival lineup — the number of owners who made the trip (mountain vacation in the heart of the summer!), and the presence of breWeries that rarely pour on the festival circuit, including Denver’s Bierstadt Lagerhaus and Black Shirt Brewing. For those who know Colorado breweries well, the festival highlight was a chance to try the out-of-state breweries that normally don’t distribute here. Among the standouts: Arizona Wilderness Brewing Co. brought a memorable Coffee Gose and one of the fest’s best hoppy beers (a rye double IPA), and fellow Arizonans Pueblo Vida Brewing’s Circuitous double IPA. texts.full_text: ning target molecules flows, allowing hybridization between probe and target molecule in situ. The microfabricated devices described herein are capable of detecting multiple analytes simultaneously. This capability allows for diagnosis of diseases associated with genetic markers or protein expressions without the need to perform separate tests for each disease marker. The simultaneous detection can be achieved by using different reporter molecules, such as fluorescent dyes that emit light at distinct wavelengths when bound to their respective targets. The invention also provides methods for making and using these microfabricated devices in diagnosing diseases or conditions associated with particular genetic markers or protein expressions. These methods involve providing a sample suspected of containing the target molecule(s), introducing this sample into the device, allowing hybridization between any present target molecules and immobilized probes on the substrate within the channels of the device, detecting signals from reporter molecules bound to these targets using an appropriate detection means (such as fluorescence microscopy or other optical methods), interpreting these signals to determine whether a particular disease marker is present in the sample. The use of such devices for diagnostic purposes offers several advantages over techniques. These include sensitivity and specificity due to the localized environment provided by the channels, reduced reagent consumption because only small amounts are needed within each channel, decreased analysis time as multiple tests can be performed simultaneously on a single device, and potential cost savings from these factors. The abstract of US 20020037499 A1 describes an innovative approach for detecting diseases through microfabricated devices that leverage the principles of molecular hybridization. texts.full_text: you need to compete on price. After all, if you’re the cheapest, clients will flock to you won’t they? Take a look at your marketing literature: your website, your latest leaflets or flyers. What’s the main message? I met with a prospective client recently and the message that was coming across loud and clear was “we’re cheap”. This lady runs a successful business, her team work hard and they’re making a profit. But I can’t help thinking that they could be making an easier profit, attracting easier clients and making a larger profit if they just thought about repositioning themselves. Most businesses have a Unique Selling Point (USP) that extends beyond how much they charge. The trouble is, they rarely communicate that USP effectively. The downside to this? You’ll end up attracting customers who only care about price and not the value you bring. These clients might be more high-maintenance: expecting exceptional service at a discount rate or quick fixes without understanding (or valuing) your expertise, effort, and experience that goes into each project. They are also typically less loyal; if another provider offers an even lower cost down the line, they may switch over in pursuit of further savings. Instead, consider communicating why you're worth more: 1. Highlight Your Expertise: Showcase case studies or testimonials where your knowledge has made a significant difference to clients' businesses. 2. Emphasize Quality and Craftsmanship: If what sets you apart is the superior quality of work that stands head-and-shoulders above competitors, make sure this message resonates with potential customers from day one. 3. Customer Service Excellence: Position yourself as someone who not only delivers a product or service but also ensures an unmatched customer experience through responsiveness and care. By focusing on these aspects in your marketing materials—website content, brochures, social media—you can attract clients that value what you offer beyond just the price tag. texts.full_text: urce OCT, including 3D angiographic scans. Four ophthalmologists reviewed images from ten patients in a masked fashion to identify IRMAs versus NV using structural criteria on en face slabs alone as well the same data presented alongside projection artifacts removed (PAR) slices at increasing depth increments through an automated retinal slab segmentation algorithm that allows review of cross-sectional OCT and corresponding PAR slice information. Intergrader agreement was assessed with weighted kappa analysis, sensitivity/specificity analyses were performed to compare detection rates using each approach independently against a gold standard created from fluorescein angiography findings plus clinical assessment by the same four graders in consensus together on widefield fundus photos (102 lesions for IRMA and 45NV). Lesion grading results revealed good intergrader agreement between all readers regardless of whether structural or angiographic criteria were used, but overall sensitivity was significantly higher using en face slabs alone than with PAR slices. Sensitivity/specificity analyses confirmed this trend at a weighted average across graders (96%/78% vs 43-50%, p< .OOI) respectively for OCTA slab versus the use of corresponding depth information to remove projection artifacts, but it should be noted that all readers achieved excellent specificity (> or =to1.e.) regardless if PAR slices were used at depths <or=27 microns (mean range 8-360m). In conclusion; we found IRMAs can typically still show capillary dropout on en face OCTA slabs despite projection artifacts, whereas NV may be more difficult to distinguish in some cases and should therefore require confirmation of corresponding PAR slice

It would seem, from Pryderi's remark, that there is some connection between the three, yet the other two do not (if I recall correctly) appear in the Mabinogion. Is there a connection between the Three Humbled Chieftains? A: As I noted in my comment, that quote refers to a welsh triad, sometimes translated as "Three Prostrate (Humble) Chieftains of the Island of Britain": Llywarch the Old son of Elidir Llydanwyn, and Manawydan son of Llyr Half-Speech, and Gwgon Gwron son of Peredur son of Eliffer of the Great Retinue. texts.full_text: first time, or enabled comments on your blog. You carefully craft a post or two, click the submit button, and wait with bated breath for comments to come in. And they do come in. Perhaps you get a friendly note from a fellow blogger, a pressing update from an MMORPG guild member, or a reminder from your Aunt Millie about dinner on Thursday. But then you get something else. Something... disturbing. Offers for deals that are too good to be true, bizarre logorrhean gibberish, and explicit images you certainly don't want Aunt Millie to see. You are now buried in a deluge of dreaded comment spam. Comment spam is bad stuff all around. It's bad for you, because it adds to your workload. It's bad for your users, who want to find information on your site and certainly aren't interested in dodgy links and unrelated content. It's bad for the web as a whole, because the more that spammers are able to profit from comment spam, the more time and effort you'll have to spend to combat it. The good news is that there are simple things you can do today to cut down on the amount of comment spam you receive. It's important for site owners to be proactive in preventing comment spam--it only takes one or two people clicking a bad link to potentially cause damage to them, and to make more work for everyone else. ### It all starts with great content Let's start with what really works. Create awesome content! People who find your site interesting and useful are going to want to interact with it somehow, either through adding a comment on an existing article or blog post

texts.full_text: ar, and more, much more. Wednesday, April 22, 2009 Mostly Leftovers Another trip to Key Food today, and probably my last. I think I will only need to buy more bananas to get me through the end of my month. Today I bought an Oatmeal Variety Ten Pack for $1.99. The individual portions look a bit on the skimpy side, but I will try one tomorrow and see. No problem though because I still have plenty of eggs, and omelets for breakfast are always good. I also bought a bag of onions for $1.99 and two 1 pound bags of frozen mixed vegetables for $3.98. And $1.49 worth of dried bananas. They were $1.99 a pound so I guess I bought a little bit less than that. That was $9.45 for the day and a grand total to date of $137.80. I figure I should end up under $140 for the month.This morning I ate breakfast before I went shopping so I had a real banana in my oatmeal again. I took a picture of it but unfortunately, the camera phone is still not working properly. So instead, I'll just describe everything. My oatmeal was pretty good once you got past the first bite or two when all there really was was oats and banana. texts.full_text: ion South Sudan has embarked on a program to transform the Sudan People’s Liberation Army (SPLA), the country’s preindependence guerrilla army, into a professional, conventional force by 2017. However, the success of this transformation is conditional on effectively addressing one crucial issue: significantly reducing the size of the current military force. This process, known as force reduction or right-sizing, aims to create an army that is leaner and more efficient—a formidable professional defense force appropriate for a sovereign South Sudan. The rationale behind this strategy lies in several critical factors. Firstly, the SPLA's historical role as a guerrilla force may not align with the demands of modern conventional warfare. Transitioning into a professional military requires a shift in structure, tactics, and personnel—a process that can be facilitated by reducing excess manpower. Secondly, shrinking the army will help address concerns about over-militarization and the potential for human rights abuses often associated with large standing armies, especially those primarily trained for guerrilla warfare. Lastly, force reduction can contribute to economic savings for the government, freeing up resources for much-needed development projects in other sectors. The process of right-sizing involves carefully planned steps including a comprehensive assessment of security needs, restructuring military units, and voluntary or, if necessary, compulsory separation of personnel—all while ensuring that those who remain are well-trained, disciplined, and committed to their new roles within a professional army. Retraining programs and support for those leaving the military will also be crucial elements in this transformation to ensure they can successfully transition into civilian life. texts.full_text: From Higher-Quality Songs," the discussion revolves around the potential for Apple to capitalize on the growing consumer demand for higher-quality digital music. The article, published in 2011, suggests that Apple, through its iTunes Store, could introduce higher-quality audio files, such as lossless or high-resolution audio, as a premium offering. This strategy could not only attract audiophiles and music enthusiasts but also justify a higher price point, thereby increasing revenue. The key points in the article include: 1. Consumer Demand for Quality: There is a segment of the market that is willing to pay more for better sound quality. This is particularly true for audiophiles and music enthusiasts who value the listening experience. 2. Premium Pricing: By offering higher-quality audio files, Apple could charge more, creating a new revenue stream. This could be particularly effective if the higher-quality files are marketed as a premium product. 3. Technical Feasibility: The technology to support higher-quality audio files was already available, and the main challenge was the file size and the potential impact on storage and bandwidth. 4. Ecosystem Integration: Apple could integrate higher-quality audio files seamlessly into its existing ecosystem, including the iPod, iPhone, and Mac, making it easy for users to enjoy the higher-quality music

texts.full_text: of the slip sheet method aresignificant, the transition is not without its challenges. Here are a few points that the manufacturer of the packaged goods might consider: 1. Handling and Stability: Slip sheets are not as rigid as wooden pallets, which might require changes in the handling and stacking processes. Forklifts might require different attachments, and the stacking pattern might need to be adjusted to ensure the goods are stable and secure. Training of the handling and stacking personnel might be necessary to ensure the goods are not damaged and the stability of the stacks is not compromised. 2. Fragility of the Slip Sheets: Slip sheets are not as durable as wooden pallets and are prone to damage and wear. This might lead to additional costs in the long run as the slip sheets might need to be replaced more frequently. This is especially true in environments where the slip sheets are exposed to moisture, extreme temperatures, or rough handling. Consideration might need to be given to the additional costs and the impact on the overall supply chain. 3. Waste Management: While slip sheets are more environmentally friendly and are easier to dispose or recycle, the additional waste might be a concern. The manufacturer might need to consider the additional waste management costs and the impact on the overall waste management strategy. 4. Transition Period: The manufacturer might need to consider the costs and impact on the business during the transition period. The manufacturer might need to have a dual system, using both the wooden pallets and the slip sheets during the transition period, and might need to have both on hand to ensure the transition period is as smooth as possible. Any admiral serving within this admiralty or the two other Hollandic admiralties (Amsterdam and the Admiralty of de Maze) had the title of Admiral of Holland and West Frisia. The West Frisian language has disappeared from the region and the later West Frisian dialects are now slowly disappearing. Although these dialects are subdialects of Hollandic Dutch, they were strongly influenced in vocabulary and grammar by a West Frisian substratum. Early Medieval Vroonen The first inhabitants of Alkmaar, Oudorp, and St Pancras likely settled along the high beach ridges of the Vroonermeer in the 9th century AD. Better known as Vroonen, this settlement subsequently grew into a village. In the late 13th century, when the dutch conquered West Frisians, the victors crossed the village and set it on fire. The few surviving inhabitants fled the region. After a long time, people returned to Vroonen and a chapel was built. The village of St Pancras was founded around this church [1484]. texts.full_text: scml and I can't open that animation in XNA. Do I have to put it in another format? The software is Spriter. A: For one, XNA dosen't include content loading or processing capabilities for SCML files directly. The SCML file type contains data about your animations created by the BrashMonkey's Spriter (or other compatible tools), but it’s not a format that can be used natively in an engine like XNA, which is more focused on older .NET Framework-based projects. To use your animation with XNA, you will need to follow these steps: 1. Export Animation Data: Use theSpriter's export functions or third-party tools (like SpriterDotNet) that can convert SCML files into a format suitable for game engines such as textures and XML data representing animations. 2. Convert Formats: - You might want to use an exporter in Spriter itself, which often comes with options like exporting the spritesheet along with animation keys. - Alternatively, look for tools or libraries that can read SCML files and generate content suitable for XNA (e.g., a custom XML format). 3. texts.full_text: the se#1 12-07-12, 08:39 PM td4mvp2k SpursReport Team Member Join Date: Jul 2012 Posts: 633 Trade for a 76ers Big? permalink With a lot of room under the salary cap in the coming years, Philadelphia definitely has the flexibility to make a deal and still offer Bynum a nice contract extension. The other option is to deal him to a team in the Western Conference hoping to use him as a rental player for their playoff run before potentially signing with them long-term. It's possible that both scenarios could happen, but there are some factors to consider when thinking about such trades: 1) Salary Cap Flexibility: The Philadelphia 76ers have been one of the most aggressive teams in terms of acquiring talent through trades and free agency over recent years. This has allowed them to build a strong core group around their franchise player, Joel Embiid. However, they still need help at center due to injuries and lackluster performances from previous draft picks like Nerlens Noel and Jahlil Okafor. 2) Potential Trade Partners: Some teams that could potentially be interested in acquiring Bynum include the San Antonio Spurs (who have shown interest in big men before), Oklahoma City Thunder, Los Angeles Lakers, or even the Miami Heat if they decide to rebuild around Hassan Whiteside and Bam Adebayo instead of pursuing another star player. 3) Long-Term Contract Extension: It's unclear whether Bynum would be willing to sign a long-term contract extension with any team he is traded to during his prime years, but it's worth considering how much money both parties stand to gain by agreeing on such an arrangement. Distribution The majority of Pergidae species occur in South America and Australia. They are the dominant family in Australia and are one of the major families in the Neotropics. Of the 14 subfamilies, three, Pergulinae, Philomastiginae, and Perreyiinae, are common to both the Australasian and Neotropical regions, but no genera occur in both regions. In the Australasian Region, Pergidae occur In the Australasian Region, Pergidae occur in both Australia and New Guinea, and to a lesser extent in the surrounding islands, including the Solomon Islands and the New Hebrides. Australia, in particular, has a high diversity and abundance of Pergidae, with the family being the most dominant in the country. New Guinea, being the world's second-largest island, also has a rich and diverse Pergidae fauna, with many species being endemic. The family's presence in the Australasian Region is a testament to the diverse and complex ecosystems in the region, which support a wide variety of invertebrate species, including the Pergidae. The family's presence in the Australasian Region, alongside the Neotropics, highlights the importance of these regions
